Main Street Opportunity Brief

St. Thomas, VI

A public-data brief for local verification, partner conversations, and careful resource-mapping.

Support Priority Signal Unavailable 0-100 public-data signal
Data quality grade D 5.6% source completeness
Business establishments Unavailable County Business Patterns
Population Unavailable Denominator vintage Unavailable

Read this first

This project uses public data to start better conversations. It does not replace local knowledge, determine credit decisions, or rank community worth.

Public-data signals to verify locally

  • The data quality grade is D. Missing or unavailable fields should be filled through official sources or local partner knowledge before making decisions.
  • The data quality grade is D, so the strongest next step is to verify source coverage before drawing conclusions.
  • Available public data may help identify where local listening, resource mapping, or capital-navigation support could be useful.

Data quality

D

Completeness: 5.6%

Population vintage: Unavailable

Income and poverty vintage: Unavailable

FDIC SOD vintage: 2025

Unemployment vintage: Unavailable

Missing fields: population, establishments, business_employment, annual_payroll_thousands, small_establishments_under_20_pct, branch_count, median_household_income, poverty_pct, unemployment_pct, rent_burden_pct, no_vehicle_pct, no_internet_pct, cra_small_business_loans, sba_loan_count, cdfi_count, fmr_to_income_pct, two_bedroom_fmr

Questions to ask local partners

  • What types of businesses are struggling most to access affordable capital or coaching?
  • Are business owners aware of CDFIs, SBDCs, chambers, SCORE, or other local resources?
  • Are language, transportation, digital access, or documentation barriers affecting participation?
  • Are there local procurement or anchor-institution opportunities that could support small firms?
  • Which existing organizations are already trusted by business owners?

Possible starting points

  • Host a capital-readiness workshop with local partners.
  • Build a local resource guide for entrepreneurs.
  • Connect businesses to CDFIs, SBDCs, SCORE chapters, chambers, and other support organizations.
  • Help entrepreneurs understand cash-flow basics and documentation needs.
  • Coordinate with libraries, community colleges, faith communities, and local institutions.
  • Support buy-local or procurement-readiness efforts where local partners see demand.

Sources and data vintage

County values are built from the public source manifest. Optional and manual sources remain visible when absent so missing data is not silently filled.

  • available 2023 Census Cartographic Boundary File, Counties
  • available 2023 County Business Patterns
  • available Vintage 2025 County Population Estimates
  • available 2024 Small Area Income and Poverty Estimates
  • available 2023 ACS 5-year detailed tables
  • available FDIC BankFind Suite branch locations
  • available FDIC Summary of Deposits
  • manual optional BLS Local Area Unemployment Statistics county import
  • unavailable 2024 FFIEC CRA small-business lending aggregate county file
  • available SBA 7(a)/504 lending county aggregate
  • available Certified CDFI county aggregate
  • unavailable HUD Fair Market Rents / Income Limits county context
Open source details

Limitations

Public data is lagged, incomplete, and uneven across geographies. These signals should be verified with local residents, business owners, and community organizations before drawing conclusions or choosing interventions.

Main Street Capital Access Atlas

Public data to start better conversations about small-business support. Built independently; no private data.

This project is built independently using public data. It is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by any financial institution, government agency, or data publisher.
